Cysteine’s side chain ends in a sulfhydryl - side groups can covalently bond

Side group interactions:
Non-polar side groups  hydrophobic clusters
Polar side groups  hydrogen bonds
Charged side groups  ionic bonds  (opposite charges)
ionic repulsion  (same charge)
Cysteine –SH groups  disulfide bonds
